text: fat is 0.3g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, glycemic behaviour matters a ton.
carbs is 38.1g. the carb side gets messy pretty quickly. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
protein is 1.4g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin c at 34% DV, vitamin a at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side. major staple-energy role globally because tests whether practical staple legitimacy can rescue rough carb behaviour. very versatile staple because broad use cases. does bring some vitamin C because not empty on micros.

now the downsides. glycemic behaviour is brutal because a direct stress test for the tuber ruleset. fibre payoff is weak because poor for a stronger tuber. preparation matters for safety because not a trivial downside.
D tier. Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
